message:
✨ feat(analytics): projected pace lines for current-week ACCM charts (#7568)

The current (incomplete) week always shows a dip on the ACCM charts
because only a fraction of 7 days have elapsed. This makes the project
look like it's declining every Monday-through-Saturday, which is
misleading — especially for screenshots in blog posts and the arXiv
paper.

Add projected full-week pace indicators to all 5 weekly charts:

1. Weekly Activity (bar) — translucent stacked bars showing projected
   remainder on top of actual counts
2. AI vs Human PRs (line) — dashed extension line from actual to
   projected pace
3. AI vs Human Issues (line) — same dashed extension
4. CI Pass Rates (line) — current week labeled with * but no projection
   (pass rates are percentages, not cumulative counts)
5. Contributor Growth (bar+line) — translucent projected bar on new
   contributors/week

All pace datasets are hidden from the legend (filter function) so
they don't clutter the chart key. A footnote explains the * and
dashed lines. The projection math is simple: actual × (7 / daysElapsed).
On day 7 (Sunday) the projection equals the actual and disappears.

Also updates the ACCM paper link to the arXiv URL.

Signed-off-by: Andy Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
